message:
fix: use libc::c_char instead of i8 for ARM64 compatibility

On aarch64-linux, libc::c_char is u8 (not i8 as on x86_64), so
hardcoding `0i8` in the reverse_dns host buffer causes a type mismatch
compilation error. Use `0 as libc::c_char` which is correct on all
architectures.

Closes #17

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
